p.seg: Florence by the grace of God (bishop) elect of Glasgow sends greeting in the Lord to all who shall see or hear these letters.

Know us by the advice and consent of the chapter of Glasgow and the clergy of our diocese to have granted and by this our charter to have made firm to God and to St Mary and the church of Melrose and to the monks serving God in that place, in free and quit and everlasting alms, the church of Hassendean with lands and teinds and everything belonging to it, for the perpetual support of the poor and the pilgrims coming to the (monastic) house of Melrose. In addition, moved by religious compassion, we have granted to the same monks and we have directed to be made firm by this our charter, that when, God willing, the gift of our consecration has been obtained, we shall confirm to the same the aforesaid church of Hassendean with all liberties granted to them by our predecessor, Jocelin of good memory, bishop of Glasgow, by episcopal seal.

With these witnesses: R(obert) the archdeacon; John the official; Benedict the chaplain; Maurice and Master Henry our clerks; Henry the queen's clerk; Gregory of Stobbo; Theodore our nephew; Nicholas, Ingram, Stephen, Walter, Haimo, our servants; and many other persons of the diocese of Glasgow.
